revision.comment: 新しいページ：国分寺古墳（鳥取県倉吉市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 国分寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残存部（左は[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]の堂宇） |別名 = 伯耆国分寺古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府字東前（[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]境内）{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}} |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 48.60 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = （推定）墳丘長50m |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]]（内部に[[木棺]]） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 銅鏡3面・鉄器多数ほか |史跡指定 = 倉吉市指定史跡「国分寺古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Tottori |ラベル = 国分寺古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''国分寺古墳'''（こくぶんじこふん、伯耆国分寺古墳）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府にある古墳。形状は[[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]]。倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|国分寺境内の遠景}}{{small|中央に本堂、その右に国分寺古墳の墳丘残存部。}}]] 鳥取県中部、[[国府川 (鳥取県)|国府川]]左岸の[[沖積地]]微高地に築造された古墳で{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}、周囲に古墳群を形成しない単独墳である{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）境内の本堂裏手に位置し、江戸時代の本堂建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に主体部が発見・発掘され、その後の市史編纂に際しては測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}国分寺古墳 説明板。 墳形は破壊のため詳らかでなく、古くは前方後円形とされたが、市史の調査以降は前方後方形の可能性も指摘される{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。墳丘長は推定で50メートル程度{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後方部（後円部）中央部における[[粘土槨]]で、主軸を東西方向とし、長さ7.2メートル・幅1.8メートル（中央部）・深さ0.45メートルを測り、内部には木棺残欠と見られる木質が認められている。出土品としては、大正期の発掘の際の銅鏡3面・鉄器多数がある{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}。以上より、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定され、東伯耆地方では最古級の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 古墳域は[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）に倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。なお、付近では箱形石棺墓が発見されたというが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 === 来歴 === * 江戸時代、[[伯耆国分寺|国分寺]]本堂の建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}。 * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）、主体部の発見・発掘、銅鏡3面ほかの副葬品出土。その後の[[梅原末治]]による調査で前方後円墳と報告{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 * [[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）6月27日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）5月23日、倉吉市指定史跡に指定。 * 倉吉市史編纂事業による測量調査。前方後方墳の可能性が浮上（[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に『新編倉吉市史 第1巻 古代編』刊行）。 == 出土品 == 主体部の粘土槨からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 * 銅鏡 3面 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径22.1センチメートル。背面に鳳凰2羽を配し、「長宜子孫」・「□如日月」銘を有する。全国でも出土例が少ない古鏡とされる伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板。。 ** 三角縁神獣鏡（三角縁五神四獣鏡） 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径19.8センチメートル。「天皇日月」銘を有する。 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径14.4センチメートル。「吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮」銘を有する。 * 柄付鉇 6-7 * 鉄剣 3-4 * 短冊形刃付鉄板 1 * 刀子 1 * 鉄製小形鎌様品 3 * 鉄製斧頭 3 * 鉄製鍬頭様利器 3 * 鉄鏃 2 その他に朱が相当量検出されているが、一方で玉類などの装身具を伴わず、葺石・埴輪も検出されないという特徴を示す{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}。現在では出土品は国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。所有者は国分寺。1959年（昭和34年）6月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 ** （附指定）鉄剣・鉄斧頭等 一括 === 倉吉市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 国分寺古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）5月23日指定[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 国分寺古墳 説明板（倉吉市教育委員会、2016年設置） * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板（鳥取県教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=国分寺古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 32 鳥取県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=真田広幸|year=1989|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=加藤誠司|year=2002|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」 * [http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]、[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 倉吉市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こくふんしこふん くらよしし}} [[Category:鳥取県の古墳]] [[Category:倉吉市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:鳥取県の重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 国分寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残存部（左は[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]の堂宇） |別名 = 伯耆国分寺古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府字東前（[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]境内）{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}} |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 48.60 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = （推定）墳丘長50m |築造時期= [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]]（内部に[[木棺]]） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 銅鏡3面・鉄器多数ほか |史跡 = 倉吉市指定史跡「国分寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Tottori |ラベル = 国分寺古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''国分寺古墳'''（こくぶんじこふん、伯耆国分寺古墳）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府にある古墳。形状は[[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]]。倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|国分寺境内の遠景}}{{small|中央に本堂、その右に国分寺古墳の墳丘残存部。}}]] 鳥取県中部、[[国府川 (鳥取県)|国府川]]左岸の[[沖積地]]微高地に築造された古墳で{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、周囲に古墳群を形成しない単独墳である{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）境内の本堂裏手に位置し、江戸時代の本堂建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に主体部が発見・発掘され、その後の市史編纂に際しては測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}国分寺古墳 説明板。 墳形は破壊のため詳らかでなく、古くは前方後円形とされたが、市史の調査以降は前方後方形の可能性も指摘される{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘長は推定で50メートル程度{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後方部（後円部）中央部における[[粘土槨]]で、主軸を東西方向とし、長さ7.2メートル・幅1.8メートル（中央部）・深さ0.45メートルを測り、内部には木棺残欠と見られる木質が認められている。出土品としては、大正期の発掘の際の銅鏡3面・鉄器多数がある{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。以上より、築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定され、東伯耆地方では最古級の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）に倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。なお、付近では箱形石棺墓が発見されたというが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 === 来歴 === * 江戸時代、[[伯耆国分寺|国分寺]]本堂の建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。 * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）、主体部の発見・発掘、銅鏡3面ほかの副葬品出土。その後の[[梅原末治]]による調査で前方後円墳と報告{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）6月27日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）5月23日、倉吉市指定史跡に指定。 * 倉吉市史編纂事業による測量調査。前方後方墳の可能性が浮上（[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に『新編倉吉市史 第1巻 古代編』刊行）。 == 出土品 == 主体部の粘土槨からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 銅鏡 3面 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径22.1センチメートル。背面に鳳凰2羽を配し、「長宜子孫」・「□如日月」銘を有する。全国でも出土例が少ない古鏡とされる伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板。。 ** 三角縁神獣鏡（三角縁五神四獣鏡） 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径19.8センチメートル。「天皇日月」銘を有する。 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径14.4センチメートル。「吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮」銘を有する。 * 柄付鉇 6-7 * 鉄剣 3-4 * 短冊形刃付鉄板 1 * 刀子 1 * 鉄製小形鎌様品 3 * 鉄製斧頭 3 * 鉄製鍬頭様利器 3 * 鉄鏃 2 その他に朱が相当量検出されているが、一方で玉類などの装身具を伴わず、葺石・埴輪も検出されないという特徴を示す{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。現在では出土品は国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。所有者は国分寺。1959年（昭和34年）6月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 ** （附指定）鉄剣・鉄斧頭等 一括 === 倉吉市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 国分寺古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）5月23日指定[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」 * [http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]、[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 倉吉市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こくふんしこふん くらよしし}} [[Category:鳥取県の古墳]] [[Category:倉吉市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:鳥取県の重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 国分寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残存部（左は[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]の堂宇） |別名 = 伯耆国分寺古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府字東前（[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]境内）{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}} |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 48.60 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = （推定）墳丘長50m |築造時期= [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]]（内部に[[木棺]]） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 銅鏡3面・鉄器多数ほか |史跡 = 倉吉市指定史跡「国分寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Tottori |ラベル = 国分寺古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''国分寺古墳'''（こくぶんじこふん、伯耆国分寺古墳）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府にある古墳。形状は[[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]]。倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|国分寺境内の遠景}}{{small|中央に本堂、その右に国分寺古墳の墳丘残存部。}}]] 鳥取県中部、[[国府川 (鳥取県)|国府川]]左岸の[[沖積地]]微高地に築造された古墳で{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、周囲に古墳群を形成しない単独墳である{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）境内の本堂裏手に位置し、江戸時代の本堂建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に主体部が発見・発掘され、その後の市史編纂に際しては測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}国分寺古墳 説明板。 墳形は破壊のため詳らかでなく、古くは前方後円形とされたが、市史の調査以降は前方後方形の可能性も指摘される{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘長は推定で50メートル程度{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後方部（後円部）中央部における[[粘土槨]]で、主軸を東西方向とし、長さ7.2メートル・幅1.8メートル（中央部）・深さ0.45メートルを測り、内部には木棺残欠と見られる木質が認められている。出土品としては、大正期の発掘の際の銅鏡3面・鉄器多数がある{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定され、東伯耆地方では最古級の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）に倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。なお、付近では箱形石棺墓が発見されたというが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 === 来歴 === * 江戸時代、[[伯耆国分寺|国分寺]]本堂の建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。 * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）、主体部の発見・発掘、銅鏡3面ほかの副葬品出土。その後の[[梅原末治]]による調査で前方後円墳と報告{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）6月27日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）5月23日、倉吉市指定史跡に指定。 * 倉吉市史編纂事業による測量調査。前方後方墳の可能性が浮上（[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に『新編倉吉市史 第1巻 古代編』刊行）。 == 出土品 == 主体部の粘土槨からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 銅鏡 3面 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径22.1センチメートル。背面に鳳凰2羽を配し、「長宜子孫」・「□如日月」銘を有する。全国でも出土例が少ない古鏡とされる伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板。。 ** 三角縁神獣鏡（三角縁五神四獣鏡） 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径19.8センチメートル。「天皇日月」銘を有する。[[潮崎山古墳]]（[[広島県]][[福山市]]）出土鏡と同笵。 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径14.4センチメートル。「吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮」銘を有する。 * 柄付鉇 6-7 * 鉄剣 3-4 * 短冊形刃付鉄板 1 * 刀子 1 * 鉄製小形鎌様品 3 * 鉄製斧頭 3 * 鉄製鍬頭様利器 3 * 鉄鏃 2 その他に朱が相当量検出されているが、一方で玉類などの装身具を伴わず、葺石・埴輪も検出されないという特徴を示す{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。現在では出土品は国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。所有者は国分寺。1959年（昭和34年）6月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 ** （附指定）鉄剣・鉄斧頭等 一括 === 倉吉市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 国分寺古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）5月23日指定[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == 鳥取県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=真田広幸|year=1989|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=加藤誠司|year=2002|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 国分寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘残存部（左は[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]の堂宇） |別名 = 伯耆国分寺古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府字東前（[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]境内）{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}} |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 10.89 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 48.60 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]] |規模 = （推定）墳丘長50m |築造時期= [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]]（内部に[[木棺]]） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 銅鏡3面・鉄器多数ほか |史跡 = 倉吉市指定史跡「国分寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図=Japan Tottori |ラベル = 国分寺古墳 |アイコン = |ラベル位置 = top }} '''国分寺古墳'''（こくぶんじこふん、伯耆国分寺古墳）は、[[鳥取県]][[倉吉市]]国府にある古墳。形状は[[前方後方墳]]または[[前方後円墳]]。倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|国分寺境内の遠景}}{{small|中央に本堂、その右に国分寺古墳の墳丘残存部。}}]] 鳥取県中部、[[国府川 (鳥取県)|国府川]]左岸の[[沖積地]]微高地に築造された古墳で{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、周囲に古墳群を形成しない単独墳である{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[伯耆国分寺|護国山国分寺]]（伯耆国分寺）境内の本堂裏手に位置し、江戸時代の本堂建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に埋葬施設が発見・発掘され、その後の市史編纂に際しては測量調査が実施されている{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}国分寺古墳 説明板。 墳形は破壊のため詳らかでなく、古くは前方後円形とされたが、市史の調査以降は前方後方形の可能性も指摘される{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘長は推定で50メートル程度{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は検出されていない{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。埋葬施設は後方部（後円部）中央部における[[粘土槨]]で、主軸を東西方向とし、長さ7.2メートル・幅1.8メートル（中央部）・深さ0.45メートルを測り、内部には木棺残欠と見られる木質が認められている。出土品としては、大正期の発掘の際の銅鏡3面・鉄器多数がある{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定され、東伯耆地方では最古級の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は[[1978年]]（[[昭和]]53年）に倉吉市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。なお、付近では箱形石棺墓が発見されたというが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 === 来歴 === * 江戸時代、[[伯耆国分寺|国分寺]]本堂の建立の際に墳丘の一部が破壊{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。 * [[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）、埋葬施設の発見・発掘、銅鏡3面ほかの副葬品出土。その後の[[梅原末治]]による調査で前方後円墳と報告{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）6月27日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）5月23日、倉吉市指定史跡に指定。 * 倉吉市史編纂事業による測量調査。前方後方墳の可能性が浮上（[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に『新編倉吉市史 第1巻 古代編』刊行）。 == 出土品 == 埋葬施設の粘土槨からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 銅鏡 3面 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径22.1センチメートル。背面に鳳凰2羽を配し、「長宜子孫」・「□如日月」銘を有する。全国でも出土例が少ない古鏡とされる伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板。。 ** 三角縁神獣鏡（三角縁五神四獣鏡） 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径19.8センチメートル。「天皇日月」銘を有する。[[潮崎山古墳]]（[[広島県]][[福山市]]）出土鏡と同笵。 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径14.4センチメートル。「吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮」銘を有する。 * 柄付鉇 6-7 * 鉄剣 3-4 * 短冊形刃付鉄板 1 * 刀子 1 * 鉄製小形鎌様品 3 * 鉄製斧頭 3 * 鉄製鍬頭様利器 3 * 鉄鏃 2 その他に朱が相当量検出されているが、一方で玉類などの装身具を伴わず、葺石・埴輪も検出されないという特徴を示す{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。現在では出土品は国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。所有者は国分寺。1959年（昭和34年）6月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 ** （附指定）鉄剣・鉄斧頭等 一括 === 倉吉市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 国分寺古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）5月23日指定[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == 鳥取県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}}} * {{Cite [[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）6月27日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1978年]]（昭和53年）5月23日、倉吉市指定史跡に指定。 * 倉吉市史編纂事業による測量調査。前方後方墳の可能性が浮上（[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に『新編倉吉市史 第1巻 古代編』刊行）。 == 出土品 == 埋葬施設の粘土槨からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 銅鏡 3面 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径22.1センチメートル。背面に鳳凰2羽を配し、「長宜子孫」・「□如日月」銘を有する。全国でも出土例が少ない古鏡とされる伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板。。 ** 三角縁神獣鏡（三角縁五神四獣鏡） 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径19.8センチメートル。「天皇日月」銘を有する。[[潮崎山古墳]]（[[広島県]][[福山市]]）出土鏡と同笵。 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 **: 舶載鏡（中国製鏡）。直径14.4センチメートル。「吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮」銘を有する。 * 柄付鉇 6-7 * 鉄剣 3-4 * 短冊形刃付鉄板 1 * 刀子 1 * 鉄製小形鎌様品 3 * 鉄製斧頭 3 * 鉄製鍬頭様利器 3 * 鉄鏃 2 その他に朱が相当量検出されているが、一方で玉類などの装身具を伴わず、葺石・埴輪も検出されないという特徴を示す{{Sfn|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}。現在では出土品は国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。所有者は国分寺。1959年（昭和34年）6月27日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 ** 夔鳳鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 ** 二神二獣鏡 1面 ** （附指定）鉄剣・鉄斧頭等 一括 === 倉吉市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 国分寺古墳 - 1978年（昭和53年）5月23日指定[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]（倉吉市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 国分寺古墳 説明板（倉吉市教育委員会、2016年設置） * 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品 説明板（鳥取県教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=国分寺古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 32 鳥取県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|国分寺古墳（平凡社）|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=真田広幸|year=1989|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=加藤誠司|year=2002|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|伯耆国分寺古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=岩本崇|year=2006|chapter=伯耆国分寺古墳の再検討|title=大手前大学史学研究所紀要 オープン・リサーチ・センター報告 第6号|publisher=大手前大学史学研究所|isbn=|pages=123-142|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2019|chapter=|title=伯耆国分寺古墳の研究 -鳥取県倉吉市-（島根大学考古学研究室調査報告 第18冊）|publisher=島根大学法文学部考古学研究室・伯耆国分寺古墳研究会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9805|伯耆国分寺古墳出土品}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/F30D94A60FC482D14925796F0007FC6F?OpenDocument 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」 * [http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#07 社地区の文化財 > 国分寺古墳]、[http://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/01/#02 社地区の文化財 > 伯耆国分寺古墳出土品] - 倉吉市ホームページ revision.text:
{{Japanese kofun |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * **Bronze mirrors**: * **Kuihō Mirror**: A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror**: Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror**: A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Iron implements**: * Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. * Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * **National Important Cultural Property**: * Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * **Kurayoshi City Historic Site**: * Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]]

Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * **Bronze mirrors**: * **Kuihō Mirror**: A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror**: Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror**: A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Iron implements**: * Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. * Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * **National Important Cultural Property**: * Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * **Kurayoshi City Historic Site**: * Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * **Bronze mirrors**: * **Kuihō Mirror**: A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror**: Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror**: A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * **Iron implements**: * Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. * Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * **National Important Cultural Property**: * Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * **Kurayoshi City Historic Site**: * Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * **National Important Cultural Property**: * Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * **Kurayoshi City Historic Site**: * Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ** '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ** '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ** '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ** Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ** Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ** Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ** Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

{{Japanese kofun |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}} [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}} Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōki Kokubunji|ja|伯耆国分寺}} * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

{{Japanese kofun |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Kurayoshi City Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:国分寺古墳 (倉吉市)]]

The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |shape=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}}
