{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Additional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Additional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Aqqitional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone burial mound{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Aqqitional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient burial mound located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Aqqitional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. Aqqitional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (Aqqitional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A11itional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A11itional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978).
