{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also! == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |artifacts=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |burial_facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |Burial Facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=Excavated artifacts (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == Excavated artifacts from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alias=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |construction_period=Late 4th century |Burial Facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=excavations (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == excavations from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). revision.comment: another mass replace to make the parameters work correctly
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alternate_name=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |built=Late 4th century |Burial Facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=excavations (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == excavations from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] * {{ill|潮崎山古墳|ja|潮崎山古墳}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name=Kokubunji Kofun |image=[[File:Kokubunji Kofun (Kurayoshi), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining mound (left: Hall of [[Hōki Kokubunji|Gokokuzan Kokubunji]]) |alternate_name=Hōki Kokubunji Kofun |location=[[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates={{coord|35|26|10.89|N|133|47|48.60|E|region:JP-31_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped mound) |size=Estimated mound length: 50m |built=Late 4th century |Burial Facility=[[Clay burial chamber]] with wooden coffin |burial goods=3 bronze mirrors, numerous iron implements, and more |historic_site=Kurayoshi City Designated Historic Site |important_cultural_properties=excavations (National Important Cultural Property) }} '''Kokubunji Kofun''' (国分寺古墳, Hōki Kokubunji Kofun) is an ancient Kofun located in [[Kurayoshi, Tottori Prefecture]]. The mound's shape has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-shaped) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-shaped). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise shape of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The estimated mound length is about 50 meters. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == excavations from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Two Deities and Two Beasts Mirror''': A mirror (diameter: 14.4 cm) with inscriptions such as "Crafted by Ming, Blessings for Great Longevity" (吾作明怳竟大好上有東王西父王母宜子孫□□兮){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. * '''Iron implements''': ''' Iron swords, spearheads, and agricultural tools. ''' Iron axes and other tools. No jewelry or haniwa were found, but a considerable amount of cinnabar was detected{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. == Historic Designations == * '''National Important Cultural Property''': ''' Artifacts excavated from Kokubunji Kofun (1959 designation){{Sfn|National Database of Cultural Properties in Japan}}. * '''Kurayoshi City Historic Site''': ''' Kokubunji Kofun (designated 1978). The mound's type has been identified as either a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (rectangular keyhole-typed) or a [[zenpō-kōen-fun]] (keyhole-typed). It has been designated a Kurayoshi City Historic Site, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as a National Important Cultural Property. == Overview == [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hōki Kokubunji Temple}}]] [[File:Hoki Kokubunji, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Distant view of the Kokubunji Temple precinct}}]] Kokubunji Kofun was constructed on a slightly elevated area along the left bank of the {{ill|Kokufu River (Tottori)|ja|国府川 (鳥取県)}} in central Tottori Prefecture. Unlike other kofun that often form groups, this is a standalone Kofun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The mound is located on the grounds of [[:en:Hōki_Kokunbun-ji|Hōki Kokunbun-ji]] Temple, behind the temple's main hall. Part of the mound was damaged during the Edo period when the temple's hall was constructed. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional damage occurred during the discovery and excavation of the burial chamber in 1922{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The precise type of the mound remains unclear due to the destruction. While early investigations identified it as a zenpō-kōen-fun, subsequent surveys conducted during the compilation of the city’s history suggested that it could be a zenpō-kōhō-fun{{Sfn|Hōki Kokubunji Kofun (A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Neither [[fukiishi]] (covering stones) nor [[haniwa]] (terracotta figurines) have been found on the mound’s surface{{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility at the central rear portion (or circular portion) of the mound consists of a [[Clay-coffin burial|clay burial chamber]] aligned east to west. Measuring 7.2 meters in length, 1.8 meters in width at its widest point, and 0.45 meters deep, it contains remnants of wooden materials believed to be from a wooden coffin. Artifacts excavated from the chamber during the Taishō-era excavation include three bronze mirrors and numerous iron tools. == Artifacts == excavations from the burial chamber include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': ''' '''Kuihō Mirror''': A Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 22.1 cm) featuring phoenix designs and inscriptions such as "Long Life to Descendants" (長宜子孫) and "As the Sun and Moon" (□如日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}. ''' '''Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror''': Another Chinese-made mirror (diameter: 19.8 cm) with inscriptions including "Emperor, Sun, and Moon" (天皇日月){{Sfn|Kokubunji Kofun Artifact Description Board}}.
